title: Access Control Policy and Procedures
description: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an access control policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and procedures to facilitate the implementation of the access control policy and associated access controls.

title: Account Management
description: The organization manages information system accounts, including identifying account types, establishing conditions for group membership, requiring authorized approvals, tracking and managing account activations and terminations, and reviewing accounts periodically.

title: Access Enforcement
description: The system enforces approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

title: Information Flow Enforcement
description: The system enforces approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between interconnected systems based on applicable policy.

title: Separation of Duties
description: The organization separates duties of individuals as necessary to prevent malevolent activity without collusion, documents separation of duties, and implements separation of duties through assigned access authorizations.

title: Least Privilege
description: The organization employs the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks in accordance with organizational missions and business functions.

title: Remote Access
description: The organization establishes and documents usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed; and authorizes remote access to the system prior to connection.

title: Audit Events
description: The organization determines that the system is capable of auditing the events specified; coordinates the audit event selection with entities requiring audit; and provides audit event information to authorized entities.

title: Content of Audit Records
description: The system produces audit records containing information to establish what type of event occurred, when the event occurred, where the event occurred, the source of the event, the outcome of the event, and the identity of any users associated with the event.

title: Audit Review, Analysis, and Reporting
description: The organization reviews and analyzes system audit records to identify inappropriate or unusual activity, and reports findings to appropriate personnel.

title: Audit Generation
description: The system provides audit record generation capability for the events defined and allows authorized personnel to select which events are audited.

title: Security Assessments
description: The organization develops a security assessment plan describing the scope, including the security controls and controls under assessment, assessment procedures to be used, and the assessment environment.

title: Continuous Monitoring
description: The organization develops a continuous monitoring strategy and implements a monitoring program that includes establishing monitoring frequencies and assessing security controls continuously.

title: Baseline Configuration
description: The organization develops, documents, and maintains under configuration control a current baseline configuration of the system.

title: Configuration Change Control
description: The organization determines the types of changes to the system that are configuration-controlled; reviews proposed changes; approves changes with explicit consideration of security impacts; and documents changes.

title: Configuration Settings
description: The organization establishes and documents configuration settings for information technology products employed within the system using security configuration checklists; implements the configuration settings; and monitors for unauthorized changes.

title: System Component Inventory
description: The organization develops and documents an inventory of system components that accurately reflects the current system, and includes all components within the authorization boundary.

title: Contingency Plan
description: The organization develops a contingency plan for the system that identifies essential missions and functions and associated contingency requirements; provides recovery objectives and priorities; and addresses contingency roles and responsibilities.

title: Identification and Authentication
description: The system uniquely identifies and authenticates organizational users or processes acting on behalf of users.

title: Authenticator Management
description: The organization manages system authenticators including verifying identity before establishing authenticators; changing default authenticators; establishing minimum and maximum lifetime restrictions; and changing or refreshing authenticators periodically.

title: Incident Handling
description: The organization implements an incident handling capability for the system that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.

title: Incident Monitoring
description: The organization tracks and documents system security incidents.

title: Risk Assessment
description: The organization conducts a risk assessment to identify threats to and vulnerabilities in the system; determines the likelihood and impact of adverse events; and documents risk assessment results.

title: Vulnerability Scanning
description: The organization scans for vulnerabilities in the system and hosted applications; employs vulnerability scanning tools that facilitate interoperability and update the tools with new vulnerabilities; and analyzes scan reports.

title: System Development Life Cycle
description: The organization manages the system using a system development life cycle that includes information security considerations; defines and documents security roles and responsibilities; and integrates security into the life cycle.

title: Boundary Protection
description: The system monitors and controls communications at the external boundary of the system and at key internal boundaries within the system; and connects to external networks or systems only through managed interfaces.

title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
description: The system protects the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted information.

title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
description: The organization establishes and manages cryptographic keys for required cryptography employed within the system.

title: Cryptographic Protection
description: The system implements cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of information and detect changes to information during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical measures.

title: Protection of Information at Rest
description: The system protects the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.

title: Flaw Remediation
description: The organization identifies, reports, and corrects system flaws; tests software updates related to flaw remediation for effectiveness and potential side effects; and installs updates within a time period based on the organization-defined risk.

title: Malicious Code Protection
description: The organization employs malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points to detect and eradicate malicious code; updates the mechanisms as new releases become available; and configures the mechanisms for periodic scanning.

title: System Monitoring
description: The organization monitors the system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks; identifies unauthorized use; and monitors inbound and outbound communications traffic for patterns of anomalous activity.

title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
description: The organization employs integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information.

title: Information Management and Retention
description: The organization manages and retains information within the system and information output from the system in accordance with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and operational requirements.

title: Physical Access Authorizations
description: The organization develops and keeps current a list of personnel with authorized access to the facility where the system resides; issues authorization credentials; and controls physical access to the facility.

title: Media Access
description: The organization restricts access to system media to authorized personnel.

title: Security Awareness Training
description: The organization provides basic security awareness training to all system users as part of initial training and refresher training when required.

title: Rules of Behavior
description: The organization establishes and makes readily available to all system users the rules that describe their responsibilities and expected behavior with regard to information and system usage.

title: Position Risk Designation
description: The organization assigns a risk designation to all positions; establishes screening criteria for individuals filling those positions; and reviews and updates position risk designations.
